title: caltha palustris f. radicans
intro: Caltha palustris f. radicans, also known as marsh marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of caltha palustris f. radicans include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or salves, and can be applied topically to affected areas or taken internally in moderation.
